reviews.review_title: 2 cents from a non artist vr enthusiast
reviews.review_description:
I think its realistic enough to feel that you are with a canvas. Again not a pro :) i wish there were some features though (maybe there is and I dont know?)
1-a social painting possibility. If we could see other painters in that large area that would have been nice. Could even teach one another a thing or two. Hold events and such. I think multi-player shall be considered 
2-a way to cast training videos. As I said envr is large enough to have a wall where we could turn on YouTube videos follow Bob Ross maybe or just listen to the music at the same time.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! 1. has been our holy grail for a while, but it's really hard to let it run at the same quality on a standalone Quest. and 2. there's a resizable in-game browser that lets you watch all the BR tutorials your heart desires! Thanks for the stars! 

Wim

reviews.review_description: I love this game and have a great time playing it! It’s fairly easy to get used to and customizable. There’s some problems with grabbing items and they show up outside of your range and the brushes glitching a bit when in contact with the canvas but other than that I haven’t had many issues. I would really like to see hand tracking support come to the game, I’m not sure if it’s possible, if not then I understand but if it is it would be a nice addition to see, I would also like to see more tools like watercolors and pencils.

developer_response.body: Hey hey! Have you tried Wait What's That? It's our free Pictionary game, but you can draw in the lobby with hand tracking. Maybe a good way to figure out if it's something.

reviews.review_description: It's one of my favorite  vr art tools. I spent hours just enjoying creating  something fun.  I enjoy the different brushes and the different  ways to make and get colors. It is super enjoyable, and I have had this for some time . Every time I come back to it, there always something new that they have added to that makes you enjoy making art in their app.  My favorite is there no clean up, favorite  thing about vr art.

developer_response.body: Oooooh - thank you SO MUCH! This means everything to us, creativity is a joy for the rest of your life.

reviews.review_description: Being a dabbler in real paints, once you hit a certain level of comfort, paints, brushes, cleaners, and canvas get pretty expensive. For the price of one or two tubes of high quality paint, you can have Painting VR, allowing you unlimited canvases, unlimited paint, brushes that are pretty believable, and none of the cleanup! Even factoring in the cost of a quest 3s right now, it's still in the ballpark of acquiring a full set of professional artist grade equipment and supplies, all of which will need replacing. Painting VR easily gives me that relaxed mind set I get while painting, I don't have to worry about proper brush care or paints drying too fast, I can just paint without overthinking. Add to that a team that is very active and accessible on their discord, speedy responses in their bug channel, and we have a winner. Yes there's in-app purchases, but for the prices, the new environments all feel unique, and each brush pack adds more quality and fun. If you like even just dabbling in paint, this is probably the best app on the quest in terms of quality and ease of use. Trust me, I've purchased almost all of them, and there's something special in Painting VR. Yes there's bugs, no it's not exactly like real painting, but I believe it's the closest we can get right now.

developer_response.body: Thanks a million - this means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description: the tools really impress me. Just needs more out of the box thinking and novelty.

developer_response.body:
Even more out of the box? Do you have any suggestions? If yes, join the Discord and let us know! Thanks for the stars! 

Wim

reviews.review_description:
Such a creative and intuitive app. An artist created the artwork for my son’s birth card in VR using this app. I recorded the process and added a QR code to the printed card. After people realized the painting itself was made in VR, they could scan the code and watch the artwork being created.

A beautiful way to combine art and technology. Highly recommended. 🎨

reviews.review_description: Hey! The game is absolutely amazing! But there are a few things bothering me, first of all, you need to make a canvas that you can hold in your hand, for example, make it like a sketchbook or something that you can lock so you don’t-have to look upward. Second, make the settings a little easier to handle. Third, make more stencils that you can use anytime easily that are made in game because i was trying to paint the sigma imposter lol. Forth and last, make it easier to just let go and be able to grab things off the inventory(I’m trying to say that make it easier to pick up and let go of paint or a brush). Like I said the game is great, but those are just tips that I think would make the game top notch. Great job! 👍🏻

developer_response.body:
Hey Grandma! Thanks so much for the review  and for taking the time to share this, it really means a lot. 

Just to share a couple of things that might be useful:

• You can already create custom stencils in-game using the plus button in the stencil panel. They save automatically and you can reuse them anytime.
• Picking up and letting go of brushes and paint is supported, though it sounds like we can make that feel easier and more natural, which is really helpful for us to hear.

Also the handheld canvas or sketchbook idea is a really nice idea, thank you.

If you’re up for it, we’re most active on Discord and it’s the best place to chat directly with us, share clips, and help us test things. 

Thanks again for the kind words and tips. 🙌

reviews.review_description:
After graduating from the Academy of Fine Arts, I worked at a game company. Later, I resigned due to mental health issues and never picked up a paintbrush again. This game inspired me to draw once more and rediscover the joy of gaming. I’m truly grateful to the development team for all their hard work.
 
However, there is one aspect that troubles me. The pen-holding posture in the game is fixed. In reality, most painters hold brushes in distinctive personal styles. A rigid grip makes it hard to create detailed artwork. I hope the team can add the function of free pen-holding control in future updates.

developer_response.body: Thanks for this, means a lot. Free grip is in the works!

reviews.review_title: Therapeutic, easy to get to grips with
reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love painting vr! It's really nice to be able to paint when I might not necessarily have the ability to do so IRL, whether that's because of space, funds, or whatever, so it's really nice to be able to just hop into vr and do it here.
Brushes can very rarely be a bit finnicky, and there are one or two features I would like, but I joined their server and recommended them and the devs are really appreciative of feedback.
I love how you can save them to make your own little gallery, follow tutorials as you go, and it's really helpful to be able to change like your canvas settings, hand settings etc.
This became my favourite VR game instantly!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review! Glad to hear you're  enjoying our Discord too. Happy painting 😊

reviews.review_description:
I personally think this is a great game. It feels very polished, right down to the menus and controller tracking, and it runs extremely smoothly on the Meta Quest 3. The game offers a lot of creative freedom and is accessible for both beginners and more experienced players or artists.

One thing I really like is that you’re not limited to just painting — you can also draw using markers, which makes it much easier to add fine details to your artwork. While it’s a bit disappointing that some drawing tools are locked behind additional purchases, the core gameplay is strong enough that it doesn’t take away from the overall experience.

I also want to give credit to the developers for how realistic this game feels. I’m not an expert artist by any means, but everything genuinely feels like painting or drawing in real life, right down to how the paint smudges on the canvas.

In the future, I would love to see a multiplayer update where you can paint or draw in real time with friends. That would be an amazing addition.

Overall, this is a very solid and enjoyable VR experience, and it’s definitely worth checking out.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the stars and review Xavstone. We have multiplayer on the roadmap and it should be coming later this year.

developer_response.body:
Dear Zen, have you found all the brushes in the app yet? They're under the menu button on your controller, and there are more than enough free brushes to paint whatever you want - people have painted the Mona Lisa with them. The extra brushes are for people who want to dive deeper, or do more specific things. And also - this might disappoint you - there's nothing to win, unless confidence because you become an actual painter:)

Enjoy!
